meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio trap. Learn a high-speed Claude Code skills setup using Antigravity to build professional-grade projects in record time.
content.answer_block: To overcome the no-portfolio barrier, use Claude Code to rapidly scaffold and refine production-ready projects. By integrating Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for iterative execution, you can transform a blank terminal into a polished, client-ready case study in hours, not weeks, while demonstrating modern AI-assisted engineering workflows.
content.intro:
The freelance market is brutal: clients don't care about your potential; they care about your proof. You’re trapped in the classic 'no portfolio' paradox—you need work to get work, but you have no work to show. The anxiety of staring at a blank repository while your competitors land high-ticket contracts is enough to make any developer quit.

But the game has changed. With Claude Code and Antigravity, you are no longer a solo developer; you are an engineering team of ten. This tutorial strips away the fluff, showing you how to leverage AI-native workflows to build sophisticated, complex applications that prove your technical authority to potential clients immediately.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Architecture
steps.description: Don't build a generic to-do list. Use Antigravity to architect a complex SaaS feature—like a Stripe-integrated subscription dashboard—that demonstrates real business value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Design a modular architecture for a SaaS dashboard featuring authentication, database schema, and payment integration. Output the directory structure and core technology stack choices.

steps.title: Initialize the Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the boilerplate based on your Antigravity architecture, ensuring best practices like TypeScript strict mode and clean folder structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the setup commands for a Next.js 14 project with TailwindCSS, Prisma ORM, and NextAuth. Focus on a clean, scalable folder structure.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build the core functionality one module at a time. This keeps the code clean and allows Claude to handle the heavy lifting of boilerplate logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the database schema for a User Subscription model using Prisma. Include fields for status, price_id, and current_period_end.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the Prisma schema for User and Subscription models and run the migration.'

steps.title: Automated Testing for Reliability
steps.description: Clients trust developers who ship tested code. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your critical business logic paths.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Jest tests for the subscription status check utility. Include edge cases for expired trials and active subscribers.

steps.title: Documentation and Portfolio Polish
steps.description: A great project needs a professional README. Use Claude Code to document your design decisions, proving your engineering thought process to clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for a SaaS dashboard. Include sections on Tech Stack, Architecture Decisions, and Setup Instructions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit README.md 'Update the documentation to highlight the system architecture and security considerations.'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'context.md' file in your root; Claude Code uses this to understand your project's history without reading every file.
Use 'claude config set' to define your preferred coding style (e.g., 'Functional programming, clean code, no comments') to force consistency across files.
Never blindly accept code. Use the 'claude ask' command to demand a 'Refactor for performance' review before committing.
Treat your git commit history as part of the portfolio; use descriptive, conventional commits to show you work like a senior developer.
Deploy early to Vercel/Netlify; having a live, clickable URL in your portfolio is 10x more effective than a GitHub link.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, record a 60-second Loom video walking through the code architecture. Link this video in your portfolio to demonstrate not just that you can build, but that you can communicate the value of what you've built.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' client to find you. Build the proof they need today using the exact workflows that senior engineers use to scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
